svg.text: Cached DataFrame vs Broadcast Variable — Data Distribution
Cache: each executor stores only its own partitions · Broadcast: every executor receives the full copy
P1,P2 → Exec 1 · P3,P4 → Exec 2 · P5,P6 → Exec 3 (tasks compute partitions and write locally)
● Each executor stores ONLY its own partitions — no node holds the full DataFrame
● Action 1: compute from source + write to local Storage Pool (lazy materialise)
● Action 2+: read from local Storage Pool — zero network I/O, no recompute
plan: InMemoryTableScan · executor dies → partitions lost → replayed from lineage
serialized → BroadcastExchange pushes complete copy to every executor
↓ full copy delivered to every executor ↓
● Every executor holds the FULL table — not partitioned, no subset
● Network cost: table_size × num_executors (driver pushes to all workers)
